evals.expected_output: Extract the exact token, try it as a Gmail message ID, retry once as a thread ID only if not found, and summarize the returned thread.
expectations: Extracts 18f7abc123 without passing the full URL to a Gmail tool
Calls read_email_thread with the token as a message ID first
Retries once with id_type=thread only if the message-ID lookup is invalid or not found
Does not use broad Gmail search or guessed metadata

evals.prompt: What is this Gmail conversation about? https://mail.google.com/mail/#inbox/18f7def456?attachment_id=att-9
evals.expected_output: Resolve the supported inbox URL using only the opaque Gmail token and ignore the attachment query suffix.
expectations: Extracts 18f7def456 and excludes ?attachment_id=att-9 from the ID
Uses the connected Gmail account rather than interpreting the URL as account selection
Uses at most the message-ID attempt and one thread-ID fallback
Does not claim arbitrary Gmail URLs are reliably supported

evals.prompt: Open this Gmail link and tell me what it says: https://mail.google.com/mail/u/0/#search/from%3Aalice/project-update
evals.expected_output: Fail immediately because Gmail search routes are not supported for deterministic token extraction, and request fetchable identifying information.
expectations: Does not call Gmail tools for the unsupported search route
States concisely that the pasted link could not be resolved
Asks for sender, subject, and approximate date, an RFC 822 Message-ID, or pasted email text
Does not spin through searches, pagination, or repeated retries

evals.prompt: I pasted https://mail.google.com/mail/u/1/#sent/18f7missing, but neither the message-ID nor thread-ID lookup finds it. Keep trying until you locate it.
evals.expected_output: Stop after the two exact-ID attempts and explain the likely mailbox mismatch or unsupported link token with concrete recovery guidance.
expectations: Stops after the message-ID and thread-ID lookups fail
Does not search the mailbox or retry repeatedly despite the user's request
Explains that /u/1 is a browser account slot and does not select a connector account
Asks the user to connect the mailbox containing the message or provide fetchable identifying information
